accepted.reason: Energy-rate style exp/power utility kernels are compact numerical building blocks.
Running statistics and autocorrelation loops are useful molecular-dynamics analysis kernels.
Queue-selection strategies are clean loop-based stochastic decision kernels.
Run-length extraction/normalization is a practical sequence-processing kernel.
Trapezoidal integration is a canonical numerical kernel with straightforward loop optimization.
Per-pixel sample accumulation is a core rendering workload with dense arithmetic loops.

skipped.reason: Cython set behavior tests are not suitable as standalone optimization exemplars.
Websocket framing/protocol internals are integration/protocol-specific and less reusable.
Serializer plumbing is framework-specific glue rather than generic compute-kernel supervision.
Project-specific core glue appears domain-coupled and less transferable for broad GRPO optimization data.
